1.11a: Address: first direct, 40 Wakefield Road, Leeds, LS98 1FD
Offer extensions must be requested within the last 30 days of the original offer period.
Offer extensions are granted only once per transaction.
Mortgage offers include a 7-day reflection period which the borrower may waive.
1.11b: Address: first direct, 40 Wakefield Road, Leeds, LS98 1FD
1.7: Borrowers and property owners must be identical.
Only direct security (A1 mortgage deed) is offered.
1.14: Firms may act if the seller is an immediate family member provided a separate partner not related to the seller acts for the lender.
Must be no conflict of interest and compliance with professional guidelines.
1.15: Firms may act if the borrower is an immediate family member provided a separate partner not related to the borrower acts for the lender.
Must be no conflict of interest and compliance with professional guidelines.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's name and address is not required.
3.1.4: Lender must be notified if the seller is unrepresented or if identity checks on the representative are unsatisfactory.
3.1.5: No alternative documents for identity verification are specified.
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's solicitor name and address is not required.
3.2.4: Notify the lender only if the seller is unrepresented or the representative's identity cannot be verified.
4.1: Valuation reports are for mortgage purposes only and must not be shared with the borrower.
If the valuer is specified as SDLAVM or SDLMDV, disregard sections 2 to 7 and 10; refer only to sections 1 and 9.
4.3: Lender aims to respond to valuation queries within 3 working days.
Do not request the mortgage advance until any valuation queries are resolved.
5.2.1: Assignable contracts and irrevocable powers of attorney in favor of intervening sellers are not acceptable.
Sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, and contract assignments to third parties are prohibited.
5.4.4: Notify the bank if Japanese Knotweed is within 3 meters of the property boundary.
If present within the property boundary, damage to outbuildings/fences must be minor.
Required documentation for knotweed: treatment plan from an accredited member (PCA/INNSA), completion certificate, and a 10-year insurance-backed guarantee.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the firm is CoPSO registered, has adequate PI insurance, and complies with the Code of Practice.
5.4.6: Local authority search indemnity insurance is accepted if the property value does not exceed £1 million.
The policy must cover at least the open market value of the property.
No other search indemnity insurance is acceptable.
5.5.3a: Private drainage systems must be compliant with current legislation.
Shared systems require legal arrangements for management, maintenance, and access.
Systems must be registered with the relevant regional agency where required.
5.5.3b: Lender does not require copies of planning permissions.
Breaches within enforcement periods require professional judgment and potentially indemnity insurance per Section 9.
5.5.3c: Lender does not require copies of building regulation consents.
Breaches within enforcement periods require professional judgment and potentially indemnity insurance.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use are only required if the property was converted from commercial to residential and planning permission cannot be confirmed.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted if they have mutual rights of support, protection, repair, and entry, plus enforceable covenants.
Indemnity insurance is required if mutual rights/covenants are absent.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are generally not acceptable.
Exceptions: coach houses with suitable insurance/garage lease, or leasehold interest with a share of the freehold.
5.8.1: Accepted for buildings converted into no more than four flats where the borrower occupies one and the freehold is owned by the borrower or another flat owner.
5.8.5: Lender accepts security where the borrower owns the freehold reversion of the other flat and vice versa.
A charge is required over both the freehold reversion and leasehold titles.
5.13.1: Deposits from unsecured credit or personal borrowing are not accepted.
Gifted deposits from close family members (spouse, partner, parent, grandparent, sibling, aunt, uncle, cousin, in-laws) are accepted if unconditional and not repayable.
Gifts from friends or employers are prohibited.
A donor confirmation letter must be obtained and dated within 3 months of completion.
Draft Declarations of Trust must be submitted 10 working days prior to completion for approval.
Clear bankruptcy searches required for all parties contributing to the purchase price.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term must be 50 years beyond the mortgage term.
Leases with less than 85 years remaining at the outset require specific valuer approval.
5.14.9: Ground rent is acceptable if <= 0.2% of property value.
Leases granted after 30 June 2022 must be limited to peppercorn ground rent.
Doubling ground rent is only acceptable if it occurs every 20 years or more and ceases after 125 years.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms may be acceptable upon referral.
5.14.12: Ground rent or service charge receipts are not required to be sent to the lender.
Retentions for service charges are permitted but must be limited to 30 days after final accounts are issued.
5.14.13: Receipted notice of assignment is not required by the lender but must be sent to the borrower.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is accepted for absentee or insolvent landlords.
5.14.17b: Lender must be notified if the property owner is a non-qualifying leaseholder under the Building Safety Act 2022.
Conveyancers must check Landlord’s Certificates and Leaseholder Deeds of Certificate.
Developer remediation requires evidence like a letter or a certified copy of a remediation contract.
EWS1 reports older than 5 years are acceptable provided no newer Fire Risk Appraisal External Wall (FRAEW) exists.
5.15.2a: Rent charges dated before 1977 require no action.
Estate Rent Charges must exclude Law of Property Act Section 121 provisions, include a 28-day mortgagee notice, or be owned by a resident-led management company.
If conditions are not met, a deed of variation is required; indemnity insurance is not accepted.
5.17.5a: The original or a certified copy of the Power of Attorney and a specimen signature must be provided before completion.
5.19.1: Lender does not provide mortgages for affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership schemes.
5.20.1: Roof space leases for solar panels must meet UK Finance minimum standards; otherwise, the case cannot proceed.
6.4.4: Financial incentives up to 5% of the purchase price do not need to be reported.
Fixed non-financial incentives (e.g., carpets, white goods) do not need to be reported.
Report: cash incentives > 5%, non-fixed incentives (e.g., a car), and part-exchange transactions.
A UK Finance Disclosure Form must be completed.
6.5.1: Vacant possession is required on completion.
Lending on let or to-be-let properties is not permitted.
6.6.4: Properties defined as Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) are not accepted.
6.7.1: Acceptable warranties: NHBC, Premier Guarantee, LABC, ABC+, Advantage HCI, Ark, BLP, Build-Zone, Checkmate, Global, Homeproof, ICW, One Guarantee, Protek, The Q Policy, Build Assure.
6.7.4: Professional Consultant's Certificates (PCC) are accepted for developments of 10 properties or fewer.
The consultant must have supervised the build from the start; retrospective certificates are not accepted.
6.8.1: Unadopted roads/sewers are acceptable if there are plans for future adoption or management company responsibility.
Lender does not require a bond or retention for unadopted infrastructure.
6.10.2: Retentions are released to the borrower. Instalment mortgages are not offered.
6.12.1: Properties with overage agreements or pre-emption rights affecting security are not accepted.
7.3: Occupant consent is required for anyone aged 17 or over (except students away from home).
Maximum of 2 lodgers occupying a single room is permitted.
Homes for Ukraine occupants do not require consent or postponement deeds.
8.1: Independent Legal Advice is mandatory for any owner not benefiting (or not benefiting equally) from the loan if that portion exceeds £50,000.
9.1: Indemnity insurance policies must be sent to the lender on completion.
9.2: Indemnity insurance must cover the full value of the property.
10.2b: Minimum 6 working days' notice required for funds.
Certificate of Title (CoT) must be submitted electronically.
Advance amounts must be in full pounds sterling only.
10.7: Delayed completion funds must be returned by CHAPS within three working days.
Deductions from the return amount are prohibited.
10.11: Interest is charged at the mortgage offer rate from the date of receipt until the advance is returned.
14.1.5: Original mortgage deeds or title documents are not required by the lender.
Include the MD reference on the registration application.
14.2.2: Post-completion documents (Register Completion Sheet, A2 Mortgage Deed if applicable, Indemnity Policy) must be sent to: Customer Service Centre, BX8 5HB.
16.3.1: Transfer of equity requires a new mortgage deed and registration of a new first charge.
Lender will not sign the transfer deed.
17.1.1: Redemption statement requests should be emailed to fd.mtg.support@firstdirect.com.
Redemption figures are only valid for the day of issue.
17.2.1b: Lender files the discharge directly with the Land Registry.
